title: digitalis obscura subsp. obscura
intro: Digitalis obscura subsp. obscura is a subspecies of the foxglove plant, characterized by its dark green, hairy leaves and purple flowers. The plant contains digoxin, a cardiac glycoside with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis obscura subsp. obscura include improved heart function, increased cardiac output, and reduced symptoms of heart failure. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and capsules, which are taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
